context.title: You Don't Need a Better Prompt. You Need Selection Pressure.
context.description: Use an AI content generator long enough and your output drifts. The third option: a population of prompts under selection pressure — and an agent that builds the review app for its own output in an afternoon.
context.suggestedPrompt:
Abstract field of parallel vertical rules of varying length
suggesting a population of candidate measures, parchment-cream
lines on a deep cool ink-near-black background with one single
signal-green chartreuse rule offset from the rest — the selected
champion emerging from the population, faint horizontal lineage
tethers connecting clusters of lines in genealogical groups,
controlled asymmetric balance, compositional weight in the upper
two-thirds with quiet open measure below, editorial publication
abstraction evoking selection pressure over a typographic
population, publication-dark atmosphere, no text no words no
letters no typography no labels no people no objects
context.notes: Editorialcontrol dispatch on selection pressure over a population of prompts. Seeded from the 'margin-rules' template but bespoked to express the thesis: many parchment rules + one chartreuse champion offset, with faint lineage tethers suggesting genealogical groups. Other candidates worth iterating toward: proof-constellation (scattered proof-mark glyphs, one chartreuse), ink-bleed-marginalia (chartreuse margin annotations).
